entities.name: ChatGPT

entities.name: LLM Arena

evidence.quote: And the problem is, again, because all of these frontier labs, they kind of have to pay attention to PR because their sales team, when they're trying to sell to all these enterprise customers, those enterprise customers will say, "Oh, well, but your model's only number five on LLM Arena, so why should I buy it?"
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

entities.name: Sora

entities.name: Claude

evidence.quote: I was asking Claude to help me drop an email the other day.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: If you look at Claude, it's been so much better at coding and at writing than any other model for so long.

entities.name: 基准测试 (benchmark)
entities.how_described: 本集被强烈批判的概念;嘉宾认为现有基准测试要么本身有错,要么因有明确答案而易被模型「爬山优化」,与现实世界脱节,正在带偏 AI 发展。

evidence.quote: The other part of it though is it's like by optimizing for the benchmark instead of optimizing for the real world, you will just naturally climb on the benchmark and, yeah, it's basically another form of gaming it.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

entities.name: 后训练 (post training)
entities.how_described: 本集核心概念之一;被描述为一门艺术而非纯科学,需要研究者的品味与判断力,以决定模型应擅长什么、看重什么样的数据。

evidence.quote: One of the things I often think about is that there's a... It's almost like there's an art to post training.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: And there's all these notions of taste sophistication that you have to decide between when you're designing your post training mix, and so that matters as well.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: This is something that you're big on and something I'm hearing more and more is just becoming a big deal in the world of post-training.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: What's kind of like been the steps along the way from the first post-training that has most helped models advance?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 强化学习环境 (RL environment)
entities.how_described: 本集认为这是模型学习的下一个前沿;本质是模拟现实的复杂长期环境(如虚拟初创公司突发宕机),能暴露模型在端到端任务中的灾难性弱点。

evidence.quote: And let me explain what an RL environment is.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: An RL environment is essentially a simulation of real world.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: And so this expert now, instead of just sitting around writing rubrics, they're designing this RL environment.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 目标函数 (objective function)
entities.how_described: 本集升华的核心哲学概念;嘉宾提出「你就是你的目标函数」,呼吁选择能真正衡量改善人类生活、推动进步的复杂目标,而不是点击与点赞的代理指标。

evidence.quote: So long story short, I think there's all these different factors, and certainly the data is a big part of it, but it's also like what is the objective function that you're trying to optimize your model towards?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: So what I'm hearing is AGI is being slowed down by these, basically the wrong objective function, these labs paying attention to the wrong basically benchmarks and evals.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And then all of a sudden it goes down and the objective function is figure out why.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Yeah, so the objective function might be... Or the goal of the task might be, okay, go figure out why and fix it.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: And so the objective function might be, it might be passing a series of unit tests, it might be writing a document like maybe it's a retro containing certain information that matches exactly what happened, there's all these different rewards that we might give it that determine whether or not it's succeeding, and so the models, we're basically teaching the models to achieve that reward.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: So I think one of the things I often say is you are your objective function.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

entities.name: 数据质量 (quality)
entities.how_described: 本集的基石概念;指出大多数人根本不懂质量,以为靠堆人力就行,但实际上它极其主观复杂(如评判一首诗的好坏),需要构建极复杂的多信号系统来捕捉。

evidence.quote: People don't understand what quality even means in this space.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: I always thought it was really important for us to have early customers who were really aligned with what we were building, and who really cared about having really high quality data, and really understood how that data would make their AI models so much better because they were the ones helping us.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: What you always talk about is the quality has been the big reason you guys have been so successful, the quality of the data.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: What does it take to create higher quality data?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: I think most people don't understand what quality even means in this space.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: What makes it a good, high-quality poem?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: If you don't think deeply about quality, you'll be like, "Is this a poem?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: That's what we are thinking about when we think about high quality poem.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: There are a thousand ways to write a poem about the moon, and in each one, gives you all these different insights into language, and imagery, and human expression, and I think thinking about quality in this way is really hard, it's hard to measure.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: And it's really hard, and so to build all this technology to measure it, but I think that's exactly what we want AI to do, and so we have these really deep notions about quality that we're always trying to try and achieve.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: So what I'm hearing is there's kind of just going much deeper in understanding what quality is within the verticals that you are selling data around.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: So you want to remove all the spam, all the just low quality content, all the pages that don't load, and so it's almost like a content moderation problem.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: Is it just the quality of the data they trained on or is there something else?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And so we stayed focused on our mission from day one, pushing that frontier of high quality complex data, and I've always loved that because I think startups...
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: And so they're also working on all these training techniques and evaluation of our own data sets to improve our data operations and the internal data products that we have that determine what makes something good quality.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: So I was always this huge believer in the need for high quality data, and then GPT-3 came out in 2020.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: We have these really unique perspectives on data, and language, and quality, and how to measure all of this, and how to ensure it's all going on the right path.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: Instead, there's a million different ways to think about what makes a high quality translation, which makes a lot of ways I think about data and quality in LLMs.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

entities.name: 爬山优化 (hill-climb)
entities.how_described: 本集用来批评模型针对特定基准测试进行局部刷分的优化方式,导致模型在榜单上分数提升,但实际能力反而变差。

evidence.quote: And the other part of it is these benchmarks at the end of the day, they often have well-defined objective answers that make them very easy for models to hill-climb on in a way that's very different from the messiness and ambiguity of the real world.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: And so it's easier for the frontier labs to hill-climb on all of these than to solve all these mess ambiguous problems in the real world.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

entities.name: vibe coding

entities.name: 合成数据 (synthetic data)
entities.how_described: 本集在讨论后训练数据取舍时被提及,指模型训练时混合使用的数据类型之一,是前沿实验室面临的无限选择之一。

evidence.quote: And then other questions like, okay, are you carrying [inaudible 00:14:49] how much synthetic data are we throwing into the mix?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

entities.name: 数据标注 (data labeling)
entities.how_described: 本集嘉宾明确表示讨厌这个词;认为它将工作描绘得太简单(如画边界框),实际上所做的工作更像是「养育人类的孩子」,教导 AI 价值观与审美。

evidence.quote: I think the thing I would end with is I think a lot of people think of data labeling as it relates to simplistic work.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: And so I've actually always hated the word data labeling because it just paints this very simplistic picture when I think what we're doing is completely different.
